hero.question: US forces enter Iran by March 31?
hero.prob_take: The 7.2% price basically prices Iran invasion as a black swan event requiring a massive escalation spiral in the next 100 days, which means the crowd is betting on either restraint or a slow burn that doesn't cross into full military intervention. Watch for Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear sites or direct Iranian attacks on US assets as the real catalyst, since controlled airstrikes stay well below the 7.2% threshold while full-scale ground invasion requires political will that doesn't exist.

daily_take.headline: Iran War Odds Falling. Market Thinks Escalation Dies Here.
daily_take.deck: US-Iran military action dropped half a point in 24 hours, now sitting at 7.2%. The market is pricing in cooler heads despite maximum tension. But 36M in volume suggests serious money disagrees with the consensus fade. Smart bettors should ask whether the market is discounting tail risk too aggressively or if the recent escalation cycle really is exhausted.
